mnemonics: Every Great Regex Employs Powerful Patterns - EGREP
keyPoints: egrep is an extended version of grep that supports meta-characters like +, ?, |, and () without escape
It uses Extended Regular Expressions (ERE) for more powerful pattern matching
Common usage: egrep 'pattern' filename(s) to search for patterns in files
commonMistakes: Confusing basic grep syntax with egrep extended syntax (e.g., forgetting + doesn't need escaping in egrep)
Using basic regex special characters without understanding they work differently in egrep
Not properly quoting patterns with spaces or special shell characters
